returned.design_summary: C3 is a narrow onboarding/product-cleanliness scrub plus proof-pin design. The work should remove current-machine residue from newcomer-facing surfaces, keep the Korean-first operating voice while adding English equivalents where requested, add a neutral charter template without changing project/ history, and add a checker-backed no-residue guard. This return is support evidence only, not source truth, not quality judgment, and not Movement authority.
edge_cases: README may keep a working example, but the checker allowance should be exact and labeled so a new hardcoded org in the primary command still REDs.
Historical Smith references that are not environment residue are a separate class; the requested pin should target /Users/smith and hardcoded org literals, not every occurrence of the word Smith.

If install.sh is run from an already-cloned checkout, no repo slug is needed; if it must clone into an absent BRICK_HOME, it should fail with an explicit BRICK_REPO_SLUG/{OWNER} instruction rather than guessing.
A new brick/templates/projects/ directory would require admission work; avoid it for C3 unless Smith explicitly opens that surface.
The current sandbox blocks tempdir-backed checker execution, so downstream proof should be rerun in a writable-temp checkout/copy.
evidence_refs: Commands run: rg scans for /Users/smith and insightwavesmith; git status --short --branch; git diff --stat for inspected files; install.sh --help; onboard.py --help; full check_profile --all attempted and blocked before execution by no usable temporary directory.
invariants: Korean primary wording stays where it exists; English is additive.
No credential, token, auth body, provider session, or raw provider state is added or printed.
No checker green or model review should be described as source truth, success, quality, or Movement authority.
No-Smith-residue checker must not be weakened by broad allowlists; allowances must be narrow and labeled.
The neutral charter template must not make project/brick-protocol/README.md historical dogfood text look generic or current-company-neutral.

proposed_changes: README.md: replace the primary clone command with gh repo clone {OWNER}/BRICK ~/BRICK plus a one-line note that {OWNER} is the GitHub org/user; keep a clearly labeled working example for the current private repo.

support/onboarding/install.sh: remove insightwavesmith as a default clone authority. Use {OWNER}/BRICK or BRICK_REPO_SLUG in comments/messages, and make the no-.git clone branch require an explicit repo slug with a friendly error rather than silently defaulting to Smith's org.

support/docs/spec/README.md: replace the 11 absolute /Users/smith/projects/brick-team-starter-kit references with a neutral placeholder such as <starter-kit-root>/... while preserving the described files and phase meaning.
agent/prompts/coo.md: keep Korean chain lines primary and add concise English equivalents inline or adjacent for each item, without deleting Korean wording.

Add support/docs/references/company-neutral-project-charter-template.md with the six requested slots: why_exists, why_now, direction, done_means, out_of_scope, managers. Mark it as a human-fillable reference template, not source truth and not a project_creation input unless a later Brick admits that behavior.

Add an onboarding legacy scrub kernel check in support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py, register it in support/checkers/check_profile.py, and wire it into support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml. The check should scan README.md, support/docs/spec/**/*.md, agent/prompts/**/*.md, and the installer surface for /Users/smith and hardcoded insightwavesmith outside explicitly labeled allowances.
The checker should include RED probes over mutated in-memory copies that reintroduce one /Users/smith literal and one hardcoded org literal, so the pin does not only assert current text absence.

support/onboarding/install.sh is the actual clone-first installer and already has structural lint through install_script_lint.
support/operator/onboard.py implements the wizard, doctor, provider preflight, adapter:local fallback, and optional recording setup; help output matched the documented host/options shape.
support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml already wires install_script_lint and is a natural profile home for an onboarding residue pin.
support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py already has patterns for in-process kernel checks and FIRE-style mutated probes, but the existing hardcoded-literal scan is dashboard-specific.
brick/templates/projects/ is not admitted; brick/templates/tasks/ admits .md task-source templates but is semantically task-focused, not project-charter-focused.
support/docs/references/*.md is admitted and fits a human-facing neutral reference template without opening Brick template catalog meaning.
